name: Monuments
description: A structures expansion for Twilight Imperium 4th edition, including Prophecy of Kings, Thunder's Edge, Discordant Stars and Blue Reveries expansions.
overview: An introduction page and overview, a page to explore all the cards, an FAQ page that links back to the cards, and a 'how to design your own monuments' page for other players who want to support this in their home-brew factions.

blocks.content: You can introduce Monuments easily using the included 'Cultural Monuments' event. (The Thunder's Edge Monuments event has been Omegad in this expansion to avoid terminology conflicts.

Q: What happens when I Nekro “M” the Spire of Ixth?
A: The copy benefits the Mahact player only (this is deliberate, to avoid ‘foreign token scripts in RAM’ buffer overflow errors). Sorry Nekro.

Q: Does The Panopticon just go anywhere on the map (excluding the Fracture), as long as there is no planet or anomaly there?
A: Yep. If you (not-Empyrean) want to stop it spawning Empyrean units, next turn, you should blockade and destroy it ASAP.

Q: Can I redeploy a monument with its deploy ability?
A: No, deploy (as per LRR 30) is only possible from your reinforcements.

Q: Can I place a monument twice using the new Construction primary?
A: Yep. Most of the time this won’t be useful, but the N’orr monument is deliberately designed to benefit from that choice.

Q: Hey wait The Obsidian monument has no placement text; is that a typo?
A: Nope, once Obsidian commits to the relic’s whispers that monument is no longer valid to place on any planet. Flip wisely.

The following design guidelines are highly recommended for any home brewers crafting their own monument (or alternate
monuments) for their own factions or homebrew shenanigans.

Monuments should always care where they are located. Making them easily defended by a player because they are crammed near (or even on) a home system means they just become an additional power, not an additional option, and that’s what Breakthroughs are for. You’ll note most monuments refer only to adjacent systems, or “this” system, or units in adjacent systems. This means that the choice to place them by a player is meaningful.

A monument’s placement limitation is a strong gate on its powers. If you limit something to Mecatol Rex or a legendary planet, then map randomness may significantly limits its usefulness.

Monuments ideally provide an additional strategy for a faction rather than a simple buff to its existing powers. This isn’t a hard-and-fast rule, since Sol, Argent, N’Orr ignore it a little.

Monuments are a strong incentive to take Construction (a quick way to catch up for awful construction objectives). This means that a faction that wants to take construction naturally (like Vuil’Raith or even Titans) doesn’t need an additional incentive to take it. Note therefore that eg. The Vuil’Raith monument is designed to be worse when you don’t use its Deploy ability.

Most monuments shouldn’t do anything if they’re not on the board. It’s unintuitive and confusing.
However, a few factions, such as Winnu and Keleres and Nekro (stupid Nekro) interact with the game as cards too, instead of only units. The card should tell you what it does, so just read the cards as written.

Action Cards/Festival.jpg.description: ***ACTION:*** Ready a non-home planet that contains 1 of your structures. If that planet contains your monument, place 4 infantry of 1 mech on it.
Action Cards/Festival.jpg.title: Festival

Agendas/Cathedral of Ixth.jpg.description:
**Elect non-home planet**
Attach this card to the elected planet. The owner of this planet gains 1 VP if their monument is on this planet.
When a player places their monument on this planet, they gain 1 VP. When a player's monument on this planet is removed, they lose 1 VP.
Agendas/Cathedral of Ixth.jpg.subtitle: Law
Agendas/Cathedral of Ixth.jpg.title: Cathedral of Ixth

Agendas/Minister of Culture.jpg.description:
**Elect player**
As long as the owner of this card controls their monument their commodity value is increased by 3.
The elected players gains this card and replenishes their commodities.
Agendas/Minister of Culture.jpg.subtitle: Law
Agendas/Minister of Culture.jpg.title: Minister of Culture

Monument Funding.jpg.description:
After you perform the primary ability of the "Construction" strategy card, if your monument is not on the game board, you may place your monument.
Players cannot place monuments using the secondary ability of the "Construction" strategy card.
Monument Funding.jpg.flavour:
Raise a monument.
Raze a monument.
Monument Funding.jpg.title: Monument Funding

Monuments/BR/Atokera.jpg.description: During the agenda phase, when an outcome you voted for or predicted is resolved, produce up to 1 unit in this system.
Monuments/BR/Atokera.jpg.flavour: The Deepmantle's spike drives the Atokera into a planet's heart.
Monuments/BR/Atokera.jpg.subtitle: Atokera Monument
Monuments/BR/Atokera.jpg.title: Deepmantle Interface

Monuments/BR/Belkosea.jpg.description: At the end of the strategy phase, choose and place 1 control token on a super weapon card in your play area. This card has the text abilities of each superweapon card in your play area that has 1 of your control tokens.
Monuments/BR/Belkosea.jpg.subtitle: Belkosea Monument
Monuments/BR/Belkosea.jpg.title: Armageddon Project

Monuments/BR/Pharad'n.jpg.description:
This unit is treated as a structure and a ground force.
***DEPLOY:*** During the "Commit Ground Forces" step, place this uni on a planet in a system adjacent to planet you control.
Monuments/BR/Pharad'n.jpg.title: Pharad'n

Monuments/BR/Qhet.jpg.description: When 1 or more of your infantry would be destroyed, you may exhaust this card to place those units on this planet instead.
Monuments/BR/Qhet.jpg.flavour: Even the singular Qhet corporates require a touchstone for space.
Monuments/BR/Qhet.jpg.subtitle: Qhet Monument
Monuments/BR/Qhet.jpg.title: Sacred Pools

Monuments/BR/Sarcosa.jpg.description: You may place this structure into coexistence on a non-home, non-Fracture planet adjacent to a system that contains your or neutral units.
Monuments/BR/Sarcosa.jpg.subtitle: Sarcoma Monument
Monuments/BR/Sarcosa.jpg.title: Raider Stronghold

Monuments/BR/Toldar - Dishonor.jpg.description:
If you have more Honor than Dishonor, flip this card.
After a player wins a combat in this system., they may draw a secret objective.
While your Dishonor is 8, this unit cannot be destroyed.
Monuments/BR/Toldar - Dishonor.jpg.subtitle: Toldar Monument
Monuments/BR/Toldar - Dishonor.jpg.title: Charnel Fane

Monuments/BR/Toldar - Honor.jpg.description:
If you have more Dishonour and Honour, flip this card.
This unit gains the abilities matching your Honor:
2+: **PRODUCTION 2**
5+: **PLANETARY SHIELD**
8: **SPACE CANNON 4 (x3)**
Monuments/BR/Toldar - Honor.jpg.subtitle: Toldar Monument
Monuments/BR/Toldar - Honor.jpg.title: The People's Temple

Monuments/Base+PoK/Arborec.jpg.description: When you pass, place 1 infantry on a planet in or adjacent to this system other than Mecatol Rex. If that planet contains another player's units, place that unit into coexistence.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Arborec.jpg.subtitle: Arborec Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Arborec.jpg.title: Flaah Orbitals

Monuments/Base+PoK/Muaat.jpg.description: When you exhaust this planet to cast votes, for each war sun or flagship you control in this system, and for each supernova adjacent to this system, choose: cast 3 additional votes, or produce a hit against another player's ships in this or an adjacent system.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Muaat.jpg.subtitle: Muuat Monument
Monuments/Base+PoK/Muaat.jpg.title: Glory Furnace

Monuments/Base+PoK/Vuil'Raith.jpg.description: **DEPLOY**: When you gain control of a planet that contains 1 or more structures, you may place this unit on that planet and capture each other structure on that planet as if it were a non-fighter ship.
Monuments/Base+PoK/Vuil'Raith.jpg.title: She Consumes Worlds

Monuments/DS/Kjalengard.jpg.description: Once per action, when a player wins a combat in or adjacent to this system, you and that player may each gain 1 command token.
Monuments/DS/Kjalengard.jpg.flavour: This is the most renowned of all Kjalengard bannerhalls.
Monuments/DS/Kjalengard.jpg.subtitle: Kjalengard Monument
Monuments/DS/Kjalengard.jpg.title: Krotas Bannerhall

Monuments/DS/Kyro.jpg.description:
This planet counts as a legendary planet with an attachment and a technology specialty of no type.
When this unit is destroyed, you must place it on a valid adjacent planet you control, if able. If you do, draw a relic.
Monuments/DS/Kyro.jpg.subtitle: Kyro Monument
Monuments/DS/Kyro.jpg.title: Kyro Reliquary

Monuments/DS/Rhodun Back.jpg.description:
Flip this at any time.
Other players with units in the Fracture cannot activate this system.
During combat, apply +2 to each of your units' combat rolls in this and adjacent systems if your opponent has any units in the Fracture.
Monuments/DS/Rhodun Back.jpg.subtitle: Rhodun Monument
Monuments/DS/Rhodun Back.jpg.title: Reliquat Unleashed

Monuments/DS/Rhodun Front.jpg.description:
Start with this side face-up; when a player activates this system, you may flip this card.
During combat, apply +1 to each of your units' combat rolls in this and adjacent systems if your opponent has any unit technology upgrades.
Monuments/DS/Rhodun Front.jpg.subtitle: Rhodun Monument
Monuments/DS/Rhodun Front.jpg.title: Vault of New Phrad

Monuments/Other/Drahn.jpg.description: At the beginning of your turn you may remove this unit from this planet and place it onto a different planet you control.
Monuments/Other/Drahn.jpg.flavour: It is more a reminder of Drahn influence than a true 'monument'.
Monuments/Other/Drahn.jpg.subtitle: Drahn Monument
Monuments/Other/Drahn.jpg.title: Koldan's Outpost

Monuments/TE/Firmament.jpg.description: After a player activates this system, you may replace 1 control token on one of your plots with 1 of that player's control tokens.
Monuments/TE/Firmament.jpg.flavour: Epiphany offers a meeting place for the discussion of grand strategies,
Monuments/TE/Firmament.jpg.subtitle: Firmament Monument
Monuments/TE/Firmament.jpg.title: Epiphany

Monuments/TE/Obsidian.jpg.description: After a puppeted player activates this system, replace up to 2 of that player's infantry on a planet they control with your infantry; those units coexist.

faq.a: No, there are no valid placements for this monument. You need to place it before it no longer has valid placements.
faq.q: Can I place this on a planet once it's flipped?

Monuments/TE/Obsidian.jpg.flavour: An unknown, twisted connection exists between visitors of this place.
Monuments/TE/Obsidian.jpg.subtitle: Decayed Monument
Monuments/TE/Obsidian.jpg.title: Epiphany Hollow

Objectives/Topple Monument.jpg.description: Destroy another player's monument during a tactical action.
Objectives/Topple Monument.jpg.title: Topple Monument
